fullTitle: Policy Document on Risk Management in Technology — Cloud Services Requirements
verificationNote: Cloud requirements paraphrased from BNM RMiT (November 2025). Obtain authoritative text from BNM website. Clause references (e.g. 10.50, 17.1) correspond to the official document. Appendix 10 content is indicative — obtain from BNM.
jurisdiction: Malaysia — mandatory for all BNM-regulated financial institutions
scope: RMiT establishes comprehensive technology risk management requirements for Malaysian financial institutions. Cloud-specific requirements span two primary sections: S10 (Technology Operations Management, clauses 10.50-10.52) covering operational cloud risk and controls, and S17 (Consultation and Notification related to Cloud Services and Emerging Technology, clauses 17.1-17.5) covering regulatory engagement for cloud adoption. Additional cloud-relevant requirements appear across S9 (Risk Management), S11 (Cybersecurity), S13 (Technology Audits), and S14 (External Party Assurance).
applicability: All financial institutions licensed or registered under BNM — commercial banks, Islamic banks, investment banks, insurers, takaful operators, development financial institutions, payment system operators, and prescribed institutions. Applies regardless of whether cloud is used for critical or non-critical systems, though requirements are more stringent for critical systems.
relatedRepoDescription: Full RMiT structured compliance database — 121 clauses, 11 sections, 365 artifacts, 93 controls

cloudSections.name: S10: Cloud Services (Operational)
cloudSections.description: Operational requirements for cloud adoption — risk assessment, key risks and controls for public cloud critical systems, and data residency.

cloudSections.name: S17: Cloud & Emerging Technology (Regulatory)
cloudSections.description: Regulatory engagement requirements — BNM consultation before first-time cloud adoption for critical systems, notification for subsequent adoptions, roadmap requirements.

cloudRelevantSections.name: Technology Risk Management
cloudRelevantSections.relevance: Risk management framework must encompass cloud risks (9.1-9.3)

cloudRelevantSections.name: Cryptography (10.20-10.23)
cloudRelevantSections.relevance: Encryption and key management requirements apply to cloud-hosted data

cloudRelevantSections.name: System Backup and Restoration (10.44-10.45)
cloudRelevantSections.relevance: Backup requirements apply to cloud workloads

cloudRelevantSections.name: Third Party Service Provider Management (10.46-10.49)
cloudRelevantSections.relevance: CSP management falls under third-party provider obligations

cloudRelevantSections.name: Access Control (10.53-10.57)
cloudRelevantSections.relevance: Access control requirements apply to cloud IAM

cloudRelevantSections.name: Cybersecurity Management
cloudRelevantSections.relevance: Cyber risk management, SOC operations, and incident response apply to cloud infrastructure

cloudRelevantSections.name: Technology Audits
cloudRelevantSections.relevance: Cloud must be included in technology audit scope

cloudRelevantSections.name: External Party Assurance
cloudRelevantSections.relevance: Third-party assurance requirements apply to CSP assessments

keyPrinciples: BNM must be consulted before first-time public cloud adoption for critical systems
Comprehensive risk assessment required before any cloud adoption (10.50)
Critical customer data and backups must reside in Malaysia (data residency)
Financial institution remains fully accountable regardless of cloud outsourcing
Exit strategy must be maintained and tested for all material cloud services
CSP SOC 2 reports support but do not replace the institution's own audit obligations
24-hour incident reporting to BNM for major cloud incidents (plus 6-hour NACSA for NCII)
Annual roadmap for cloud adoption must be included in outsourcing plan to BNM
